summary: Verified Summer Camp WhatsApp Broadcast feature end-to-end. 10/10 backend pytest cases pass (templates list=7, dry-run for all/status/assignee/booking_ids, invalid template 400, live send queues + history row, add-lead with send_notifications=True succeeds, bulk-import XLSX produces unique booking_refs via atomic counter). Frontend playwright flow on Summer Camp CRM passes: green 'Broadcast' button opens modal, 7 templates load, all 4 target options render, preview count updates (35 unique phones), 'Send to 35' triggers success card 'Broadcast queued — Message template queued for 35 leads'. Backend log confirms broadcast history row inserted.

issues: CRM-status chips are <button> pills (not checkboxes) and lack individual data-testid like 'broadcast-status-<key>'. Works fine visually/functionally; adding testids would improve automation.
The assignee <select> has no data-testid (e.g. 'broadcast-assignee-select'); spec calls it out but DOM uses plain select. Functional — value submits correctly.

action_items: (Nice-to-have) Add data-testid='broadcast-status-<status_key>' on each CRM-status pill inside the broadcast modal to make automated selection easier.
(Nice-to-have) Add data-testid='broadcast-assignee-select' on the assignee <select> inside the broadcast modal.
(AiSensy) Conversation credits are 0 — actual delivery will fail with ERR402; broadcast history row shows sent=0 failed=N. Purely an external-account issue, code path is correct.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminStudentCRM.jsx is 4655 lines. Broadcast modal block (~150 lines) plus Summer Camp CRM block should be extracted into a dedicated SummerCampCRM.jsx / BroadcastModal.jsx component. Same recommendation as iteration 74.

broadcast endpoint returns target_count deduped by phone but does NOT persist the booking_ids list it actually sent to — history row only stores counts. If a retry/resend-to-failures feature is added later, consider persisting `target_ids` or `failed_phones`.
Template placeholder option in the <select> (value='') is counted as an option (total=8) — cosmetic but worth filtering out of any option-count test.

`_run_broadcast` swallows per-booking exceptions but `await send_whatsapp_notification` is sequential; with >500 targets this will be slow. Consider asyncio.gather with a small semaphore (e.g. 10 concurrent) for future scale.
seed_data_creation: Added 3 bulk-imported test bookings (TEST_Bulk_<ts>_0..2) and 1 add-lead booking (TEST_Broadcast_<uuid>) via the live API as part of backend tests. Unique phones, won't collide with future runs (uses epoch-stamped phones). Triggered 2 live broadcasts with template summercamp_followup (target=1 each via booking_ids) — 2 new rows in summer_camp_broadcasts collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Broadcast feature verified. Endpoints: GET /api/summer-camp/broadcast/templates (7 keys: summercamp_followup, summercamp_payment_pending{,_2,_3}, summercamp_phone_captured_24h, summercamp_closing_7days, summercamp_enrolled); POST /api/summer-camp/broadcast (BroadcastRequest: template_key, crm_statuses?, assigned_to?, center?, batch_week?, booking_ids?, dry_run?) — returns {success, target_count, dry_run} for dry_run=True; returns {success, target_count, queued, message} for live and fires background_tasks._run_broadcast which logs history in summer_camp_broadcasts. GET /api/summer-camp/broadcast/history?limit=N returns {runs: [{id, template_key, target_count, sent, failed, started_at, finished_at, triggered_by}]}. Frontend testids: broadcast-whatsapp-btn, broadcast-template-select, broadcast-target-{filtered|all|status|assignee}, broadcast-send-btn, close-broadcast-modal, broadcast-preview-count. The 'By status' and 'By assignee' inner controls lack testids (see design_issues). Admin login route: /admin (redirects to /admin/login when not authed). Dataset: 70 summer camp bookings; dry-run target=all gives 35 unique phones (phone dedupe). AiSensy has 0 credits so sent column will be 0 on history rows, but the endpoints and UI flow are correct. Bulk-import endpoint expects multipart/form-data file=<xlsx>, not JSON. Live broadcast to all 35 leads was NOT executed to avoid burning future credits if any are topped up — only single-booking live sends were tested.
